member.name: T:MudBlazor.CategoryAttribute
member.summary: Specifies the name of the category in which to group the property of a MudBlazor component when displayed in the API documentation.
member.remarks:
Use this attribute together with the .
This attribute is similar to .
The name of the category can be specified by using a constant defined in the class.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.CategoryAttribute.Name
member.summary: The name of the category.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.CategoryAttribute.Order
member.summary: The order of the category - the greater the number the lower the category will be displayed in the API documentation.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.CategoryTypes
member.summary: Possible categories of MudBlazor components properties.

remarks.para:
- Data - Used e.g. in form fields, pickers, , , , , and . Containers have this group when their items can be defined not only in markup language, but also programmatically in the Items property and by specifying ItemTemplate.
- Validation - Used in form fields and pickers.
- Behavior - Changing these properties changes behavior of the component and behavior of the application. So in some way they are or may be more important than the "Appearance" category. Example properties are: a) the Disabled property, b) icons (or avatars) without default value (because setting their value can pass additional information).
- Appearance - Changing these properties doesn't change behavior of the component and behavior of the application, but only changes the appearance of the component irrelevant to the understanding of the application by a user. So in some way they are less important than the "Behavior" category, because they are only used to adjust the look of the application. Example properties are: a) Elevation, Outlined, Square, Rounded, Gutters, Dense, Ripple; b) size, color, and typography of the item and its subelements; c) CSS classes and styles of subelements; d) icons with the default value already set (because most often changing its value doesn't change passed information).
- Common - Properties defined in .

Note: The following properties belong to the "Behavior" group, not to the "Appearance" group:
- - because it describes meaning of the button, since MudIconButton doesn't have text,
- - because it describes meaning of the field,
- - because it can describe information important to a user, e.g. a numeric field unit such as kilograms.
Sometimes choosing a category can be difficult - in such case choose a category that makes the most sense.
remarks.b: Categories for specific behaviors or specific parts of components
remarks.para: If some elements or behaviors can be distinguished in a component, then their properties are included in separate groups.

Note: If a property qualifies for both the "Appearance" or "Behavior" group, and for some special group, then this special group takes precedence. For example, could belong to the "Appearance" group, but belongs to the "Editing" group.
The list of categories is inspired by the categories displayed for Windows Forms and Web Forms components in the Visual Studio "Properties" window.

member.summary: Resolves which parameter is effective when coordinating changes between two related parameters.
member.typeparam: The type of the first parameter.
The type of the second parameter.
member.param: The state object for the first parameter.
The state object for the second parameter.
The name of the parameter that should take precedence if both parameters have changed.
member.returns: An indicating which parameter is effective, including its name and value.
member.remarks: This method compares the current and previous values of both parameters, determines which (if any) has changed, and applies precedence rules to select the effective parameter. If both parameters have changed and are non-null, the parameter specified by is selected.
member.exception: Thrown if does not match either parameter name when both have changed.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.State.Invocation.IParameterStateInvocationSnapshot
member.summary: Represents a read-only, immutable snapshot of a parameter's invocation state.

remarks.para: This interface exists to decouple parameter change invocation logic from the live parameter state. Instead of directly invoking during a render or lifecycle update, a snapshot is created containing all necessary data to safely execute the handler later.
This ensures that concurrent lifecycle operations, reentrant parameter updates, or asynchronous component updates cannot interfere with a live state mutation.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.State.ParameterState`1
member.summary: The automatically manages parameter value changes for as part of MudBlazor's ParameterState framework. For details and usage please read CONTRIBUTING.md
member.remarks: You don't need to create this object directly. Instead, use the "MudComponentBase.RegisterParameter" method from within the component's constructor.
member.typeparam: The type of the component's property value.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.State.ParameterState`1.Value
member.summary: Gets the current logical value tracked by the .

remarks.para: When you call the is updated immediately in the local instance so subsequent reads within the same render/logic will see the new value.
The represents the child component's current notion of the parameter. It may differ from until the parent re-renders and supplies the updated value (see ).

member.name: P:MudBlazor.State.ParameterState`1.RenderValue
member.summary: Gets the value most recently received from the parent component's render.

remarks.para: represents the parameter value as provided by the parent component during the last parameter set/render. It is the value that should be shown to the user when you want to reflect what the parent actually rendered.

Example: when you call the local will change immediately, but will only update if the parent component participates in two-way binding (see ). In two-way binding the child typically invokes the and the parent responds by updating the parameter and re-rendering; when that happens the child will receive the new value from the parent and will be updated accordingly.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.State.ParameterState`1.HasCallback
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this parameter has an associated on the component.

remarks.para: NB! Having a callback doesn't always mean the usage of Blazor's @bind- syntax.
When is true, calling triggers the associated so the parent can update its value and re-render — which in turn updates when the parent supplies the new value.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.State.ParameterState`1.IsInitialized
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the object is initialized.
member.remarks: This property is true once the method is called; otherwise, false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.State.ParameterState`1.InitialValue
member.summary: Gets the initial value of the parameter at the time is called.
member.remarks: This value is captured once when the component is initialized and never changes thereafter, even if the property changes later.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.State.ParameterState`1.SetValueAsync(`0)
member.summary: Set the parameter's value.
member.remarks:
Note: you should never set the parameter's property directly from within the component. Instead, use on the object.
When the parameter has an associated callback ( is true), calling this method will invoke the associated so the parent can update its state and re-render. The child's will then be updated when the parent supplies the new value on the next render.
When is false, invoking updates the child's local but does not notify the parent; therefore will only change when the parent explicitly supplies a different value.
member.param: New parameter's value.
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.State.ParameterStateInternal`1.ExtractComparer(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.ParameterView)
member.summary: Extracts the appropriate equality comparer for the parameter from the provided .
member.param: The containing the incoming parameter values.
member.returns: The equality comparer to use for comparing parameter values. Returns either the updated comparer from the if available, or the current comparer stored in .
member.remarks: This method handles a special edge case where both a parameter and its associated comparer parameter change simultaneously in Razor syntax. Since Blazor calls SetParameterProperties after this method executes, the new comparer may not yet be set in the component. This method manually extracts the updated comparer from the to ensure uses the correct comparer instead of a stale one.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.Touched
member.summary: Indicates whether the user has interacted with this input or the focus has been released.
member.remarks: Defaults to false. When true, the user has performed input, or focus has moved away from this input. This property is typically used to show the text only after the user has interacted with this input.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.ValidationErrors
member.summary: The list of problems with the current input value.
member.remarks: When using a , this property is updated when validation has been performed. Use the property to control what validations are performed.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.Validation
member.summary: The function used to detect problems with the input.
member.remarks:
When using a , this property can be any of several kinds of functions:
1. A Func<T,bool> or Func<T,Task<bool>> function. Returns true if valid. When false, a standard "Invalid" message is shown.
2. A Func<T,string> or Func<T,Task<string>> function. Returns null if valid, or a string explaining the error.
3. A Func<T,IEnumerable<string>> or Func<T,Task<IEnumerable<string>>> function. Returns an empty list if valid, or a list of validation errors.
3. A Func<object,string,IEnumerable<string>> or Func<object,string,Task<IEnumerable<string>>> function. Given the form model and path to the member, returns an empty list if valid, or a list of validation errors.
4. A object.

member.summary: Sets the culture used for parsing and formatting values in this component.
member.remarks: This method updates the parameter. The converter will automatically use the latest culture when performing conversions. If the converter supports , it will be updated with the new culture and format after the state change.
member.param: The new to use for this component. Must not be null.
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous operation of updating the culture.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.OnCultureAndFormatChangedAsync
member.summary: Called when the or format string changes.
member.remarks: Override this method in derived components to react to changes in culture or format settings. The default implementation updates the converter with the latest culture and format, but only if the converter implements . This method is typically invoked automatically when the or format parameter changes, allowing components to refresh parsing and formatting logic as needed.
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.OnConverterChangedAsync
member.summary: Called when the parameter changes.
member.remarks: Override this method in derived components to react to changes in the converter. The default implementation updates the converter with the latest culture and format, but only if the converter implements . This method is typically invoked automatically when the parameter is set or updated, allowing components to refresh parsing and formatting logic as needed.
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.GetCulture
member.summary: Returns the used for parsing and formatting values in this component.
member.remarks:
Override this method in derived components to provide a custom culture for value conversion and display. The culture is typically used by the converter (see with ) to control how values are parsed from and formatted to strings, such as date, time, or numeric formats.
By default, this method returns the value of the parameter, which is tracked by the component's state.
member.returns: The to be used for parsing and formatting values.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.GetFormat
member.summary: Returns the format string used for parsing and formatting values in this component.
member.remarks:
Override this method in derived components to provide a custom format string for value conversion and display. The format string is typically used by the converter (see with ) to control how values are parsed from and formatted to strings, such as date, time, or numeric formats.
By default, this method returns null, which means the converter will use its default formatting behavior.
member.returns: A format string to be used for parsing and formatting values, or null to use the default format.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.GetDefaultConverter
member.summary: Returns the default converter used to convert between the component's model type and value type .

remarks.para: This method is used when no custom is provided (that is, when is null). Derived components must override this method to supply an appropriate default converter for their value type. The converter is responsible for converting between the model value and the input value, including parsing, formatting, and handling culture-specific conversions if needed via .

This method is called at most once per component instance: it is invoked the first time a default converter is needed (when is null), and the returned converter is cached and reused for all subsequent operations. Do not include conditional logic that depends on per-call state or parameters.

member.returns: An instance that performs the default conversion for this component.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.ConvertGet(`1)
member.summary: Converts the specified input value of type to the model value of type .
member.remarks: This method uses the current (or the default converter if none is set) to convert the input value from the component's value type to its model type. If conversion fails, the error is captured and can be accessed via and .
member.param: The input value to convert.
member.returns: The converted value of type , or null if conversion fails.
member.exception: Thrown if returns null, which indicates a programming error in the derived component class.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.ConvertSet(`0)
member.summary: Converts the specified model value of type to the component's value type .
member.remarks: This method uses the current (or the default converter from if none is set) to convert the model value to the component's value type. If conversion fails, the error is captured and can be accessed via and .
member.param: The model value to convert.
member.returns: The converted value of type , or null if conversion fails.
member.exception: Thrown if returns null, which indicates a programming error in the derived component class.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudFormComponent`2.GetConverter
member.summary: Gets the converter used to convert between the component's model type and value type .
member.remarks: If the property is set, this method returns it. Otherwise, it returns the default converter from . If no converter is available, an is thrown.
member.returns: An instance for value conversion.
member.exception: Thrown if neither nor provide a non-null converter.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudAutocomplete`1.Strict
member.summary: Selects items without resetting the Value.
member.remarks: Defaults to true. When true, selecting an option will trigger a with the current Text. Otherwise, an empty string is passed which can make it easier to view and select other options without resetting the Value. When false, T must either be a record or override the GetHashCode and Equals methods.

member.summary: The debounce interval, in milliseconds.

member.summary: Represents the default options for a chart with axes.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Charts.DefaultAxisChartOptions.YAxisTicks
member.summary: The spacing between vertical tick marks.
member.remarks: Defaults to 20. Value must be greater than or equal to 1.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Charts.DefaultAxisChartOptions.MaxNumYAxisTicks
member.summary: The maximum allowed number of vertical tick marks.
member.remarks: If the number of ticks calculated exceeds this value, the tick marks will automatically be thinned out. Value must be greater than or equal to 1.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Charts.DefaultAxisChartOptions.YAxisSuggestedMax
member.summary: The maximum value for the vertical axis.
member.remarks: This value is used only if all data points are less than or equal to it. If any data point exceeds this value, the Y-axis maximum will automatically adjust to fit the data. If this value is null, the Y-axis maximum will be calculated automatically.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Charts.DefaultAxisChartOptions.YAxisFormat
member.summary: The format applied to numbers on the vertical axis.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Charts.DefaultAxisChartOptions.YAxisToStringFunc

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Charts.MudAxisChartBase`2
member.summary: Serves as the base class for axis-based charts, providing core functionality for rendering and managing chart elements such as axes, grids, and legends.
member.remarks: Provides foundational features for axis-based charts, including support for shared data regions, overlay charts, and dynamic resizing.
member.typeparam: The type of numeric values used by the chart
The type of chart options used to configure the chart's behavior and appearance.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Charts.MudAxisChartBase`2.SharedData
member.summary: Gets the rectangle that defines the plot area of the chart.
member.remarks: The plot area is the region of the chart where data is visualized, excluding axes, labels, and other chart elements. Values are shared between the overlay and the main chart.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.ChartType
member.summary: Indicates the type of chart to display.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.Donut
member.summary: Data is displayed as a hollow circle.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.Line
member.summary: Data is displayed as connecting lines.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.Pie
member.summary: Data is displayed as a portion of a circle.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.Bar
member.summary: Data is displayed as rectangles.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.StackedBar
member.summary: Data is displayed as connected rectangles.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.Timeseries
member.summary: Data is displayed as connecting lines or as areas.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.HeatMap
member.summary: Data is displayed as a heatmap. Similar to how github works.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.Rose
member.summary: Data is displayed as sectors of varying radii on a circular chart.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.Radar
member.summary: Data is displayed on multiple axes originating from a central point.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ChartType.Sankey
member.summary: Data is displayed as nodes connected by edges.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MudChart`1
member.summary: Represents a graphic display of data values in a line, bar, stacked bar, pie, heat map, or donut shape.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.EdgePath
member.summary: Represents the path of a link (edge) between two nodes in a Sankey chart.
member.remarks: An defines the SVG path data and associated metadata for rendering a visual connection between a node and a node. It includes the computed Bézier curve or straight path defined by , and geometric properties such as the center point of the edge for positioning labels or tooltips.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.EdgePath.Name
member.summary: The name of the edge, typically corresponding to the label or key identifying the link between the source and target nodes.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.EdgePath.Source
member.summary: The source node of this edge.
member.remarks: Represents the originating (visual node rectangle) from which the flow begins in the Sankey diagram.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.EdgePath.Target
member.summary: The target node of this edge.
member.remarks: Represents the destination (visual node rectangle) to which the flow connects in the Sankey diagram.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.NodeRect
member.summary: Represents a rectangular node in a Sankey chart, including its position, dimensions, color.
member.remarks: A defines the bounding box of a node (or block) that acts as a source or target within the Sankey diagram. It contains both layout data (position and size) and presentation data (color and label).
member.param: A unique identifier or hash value for this node, used to ensure consistent mapping between nodes and edges.
The display name or label of the node, typically representing a category or entity in the Sankey flow.
The X-coordinate of the node’s top-left corner in the SVG coordinate space.
The Y-coordinate of the node’s top-left corner in the SVG coordinate space.
The width of the node’s rectangle, usually proportional to the node’s total inflow or outflow.
The height of the node’s rectangle, usually proportional to the flow magnitude it represents.
The fill color used to visually represent the node in the chart.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1
member.summary: A form input for boolean values or selecting multiple items in a list. Use checkboxes (instead of switches or radio buttons) if multiple options can be selected from a list.
member.typeparam: The type of item managed by this checkbox.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1.UncheckedColor
member.summary: The color of the checkbox when its Value is false or null.
member.remarks: Defaults to null. Theme colors are supported.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1.AriaLabel
member.summary: The Aria Label to be assigned to the checkbox.
member.remarks: Defaults to null. Used to improve accessibility for screen readers. Adds an aria-labelledby to UserAttributes.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1.KeyboardEnabled
member.summary: Allows this checkbox to be controlled via the keyboard.
member.remarks: Defaults to true. The Space key cycles through true and false values (or true/false/null states if is true). Delete will clear the checkbox. Enter (or NumPadEnter) will set the checkbox. Backspace will set an indeterminate value.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1.Dense
member.summary: Uses compact padding.
member.remarks: Defaults to false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1.CheckedIcon
member.summary: The icon to display for a checked state.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1.UncheckedIcon
member.summary: The icon to display for an unchecked state.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1.IndeterminateIcon
member.summary: The icon to display for an indeterminate state.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1.TriState
member.summary: Allows the checkbox to have an indeterminate state.
member.remarks: Defaults to false. When true, the checkbox can support an indeterminate state such as a null value, in addition to true and false.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudCheckBox`1.HasValue(`0)
member.summary: Determines whether the checkbox is considered to have a value.
member.returns:
true if a value is considered present; otherwise, false.
When is true, this returns true if is not null. Otherwise, it defers to the base implementation.

member.summary: The URL to navigate to when the chip is clicked.

member.summary: The target to open URLs if is set.

member.summary: The relationship between the current document and the linked document when is set.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker
member.summary: Represents a sophisticated and customizable pop-up for choosing a color.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.RightToLeft
member.summary: Displays this color picker right-to-left.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ShowAlpha
member.summary: Shows alpha transparency options.
member.remarks: Defaults to true. When true, alpha options will be displayed and color output will be RGBA, HSLA or HEXA instead of RGB, HSL or HEX.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ShowColorField
member.summary: Displays the color field.
member.remarks: Defaults to true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ShowModeSwitch
member.summary: Displays the switch to change the color mode.
member.remarks: Defaults to true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ShowInputs
member.summary: Displays the text inputs, current mode, and mode switch.
member.remarks: Defaults to true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ShowSliders
member.summary: Displays hue and alpha sliders.
member.remarks: Defaults to true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ShowPreview
member.summary: Displays a preview of the color.
member.remarks: Defaults to true. When true, the preview color can be used as a button for collection colors.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ColorPickerMode
member.summary: The initial color channels shown.
member.remarks: Defaults to . Other values are for hexadecimal values and for hue/saturation/lightness mode.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ColorPickerView
member.summary: The initial view.
member.remarks: Defaults to . The view can be changed if ShowToolbar is true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.UpdateBindingIfOnlyHSLChanged
member.summary: Limits updates to the bound value to when HSL values change.
member.remarks: Defaults to false. When true, the bound value changes when HSL values change, even if the RGB values have not changed.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.Value
member.summary: The currently selected color as a .
member.remarks: You can use properties in to get color channel values such as RGB, HSL, HEX and more. When this value changes, the event occurs.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.Text
member.summary: The currently selected value, as a string.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ValueChanged
member.summary: Occurs when the property has changed.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.Palette
member.summary: The list of quick colors to display.
member.remarks: Defaults to a list of 35 colors. The first five colors show as the quick colors when the preview dot is clicked.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.DragEffect
member.summary: Continues to update the selected color while the mouse button is down.
member.remarks: Defaults to true. When false, conditions like long latency are better supported and can be adjusted via the property.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.CloseIcon
member.summary: The custom icon to display for the close button.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.summary: The icon to display for the spectrum mode button.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.GridIcon
member.summary: The icon to display for the grid mode button.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.PaletteIcon
member.summary: The icon to display for the custom palette button.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ImportExportIcon
member.summary: The icon to display for the import/export button.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudColorPicker.ThrottleInterval
member.summary: The delay, in milliseconds, between updates to the selected color when is true.
member.remarks: Defaults to 50 milliseconds between updates.

member.summary: Enables tooltips for icon buttons.

member.summary: Refreshes the current color change mode.

member.summary: Changes to the specified color selection view.
member.param: The new view to display.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Utilities.Background.BackgroundWorkerBase
member.summary: Base class for implementing a long-running background worker.
member.remarks: This class provides a base implementation for executing asynchronous operations continuously or periodically in a background worker. It simplifies the implementation of scenarios where you need to execute asynchronous operations in the background. It also provides built-in support for graceful shutdown.

member.remarks: Will return if the background operation hasn't started.

member.summary: Starts the processing asynchronously. The implementation should return a task that represents the lifetime of the long running operation(s) being performed.
member.param: Indicates that the shutdown process should no longer be graceful.
member.returns: A that represents the long running operations.

member.returns: A that represents the asynchronous Start operation.

member.summary: Triggered when the application host is performing a graceful shutdown.

member.returns: A that represents the asynchronous Stop operation.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Utilities.Comparer.ReferenceCultureComparer
member.summary: Provides a reference-based equality comparison for objects.
member.remarks:
This comparer treats two instances as equal only if they are the exact same object reference. It ignores all culture properties, including culture name, formatting settings, and user customizations.
This is useful when using as a dictionary key or in a set, and you want distinct instances—even those representing the same culture—to be treated as different.
The hash code is generated using , ensuring identity-based hashing rather than content-based hashing.

member.summary: Gets the default instance of .

member.param: The comparer to use for individual elements. If null, uses the default comparer.

member.summary: Determines whether two sequences are equal by comparing each element.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Utilities.Converter.Chain.ReversibleChain`2
member.summary: A chainable, reversible converter that supports both forward and backward conversions.
member.typeparam: The input type for the forward conversion.
The output type produced by the forward conversion (and input type for the backward conversion).
member.remarks:
extends and implements so it can be used where a reversible converter is required. Use the reversible composition helpers to append further reversible steps:
— compose another reversible converter while preserving the ability to convert back.
— append a delegate-based reversible step by providing both forward and backward delegates; the composed result remains reversible.
Note: the base overload is still available (inherited from ) but appending a single delegate-based step (forward only) returns a non-reversible and therefore loses backward-conversion capability.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.param: Delegate that performs the forward conversion from to .
Delegate that performs the backward conversion from to .

member.summary: Performs the backward conversion (from to ).
member.param: The value to convert back.
member.returns: The result of the backward conversion.
member.remarks: Exceptions thrown by the provided backward delegate propagate to the caller. For a non-throwing alternative that captures errors in a , use .

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Converter.Chain.ReversibleChain`2.Reverse
member.summary: Creates a new representing this chain reversed (forward and backward delegates swapped).
member.returns: A new reversible chain that converts in the opposite direction.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Converter.Chain.ReversibleChain`2.Then``1(MudBlazor.IReversibleConverter{`1,``0})
member.summary: Appends another reversible converter to this chain and returns a new reversible chain that represents the composed forward and backward conversions.
member.typeparam: The output type of the appended reversible converter.
member.param: The reversible converter to execute after this chain.
member.returns: A new whose forward conversion applies this chain's forward delegate followed by . Its backward conversion applies 's backward conversion followed by this chain's backward conversion.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Converter.Chain.ReversibleChain`2.Then``1(System.Func{`1,``0},System.Func{``0,`1})
member.summary: Appends a delegate-based conversion step that includes both forward and backward delegates and returns a new reversible chain.
member.typeparam: The output type produced by the appended delegates.
member.param: Delegate that maps this chain's to .
Delegate that maps back to .
member.returns: A new that preserves reversibility by using the supplied and delegates as the next step in the pipeline.
member.remarks: Use this overload when you want to append small inline reversible steps without creating a concrete . Exceptions thrown by the provided delegates will propagate to the caller.

member.summary: Attempts to perform the backward conversion and returns a describing the outcome.

member.returns: A that contains either the converted value (when is true) or error information when the conversion failed.

member.summary: Register a converter instance for a concrete input type that is known only at runtime.
member.param: The concrete input the supplied converter handles.
A converter instance implementing the appropriate reversible converter contract for . Typically, the object should implement IConverter<TSpecific,TOut> for the supplied .
member.returns: A builder typed to to continue registrations. This overload is intended for dynamic scenarios where the concrete input type cannot be expressed at compile time.
member.exception: Thrown when the supplied does not provide a compatible Convert method for .
member.remarks: Implementations will typically validate that is compatible with the supplied . If the instance is incompatible a runtime exception may be thrown by the builder implementation.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Utilities.Debounce.DebounceDispatcher
member.summary: Delays the invocation of an action until a predetermined interval has elapsed since the last call.

remarks.para: This dispatcher implements debouncing with optional leading-edge execution. In trailing mode (default), the action executes only after the specified interval has passed with no new invocations. In leading mode, the first call executes immediately, then subsequent calls are debounced.
Thread Safety: This class is thread-safe. Multiple concurrent calls to are properly synchronized.

list.item: In trailing mode: Only the last invocation's action will execute after the interval elapses.
In leading mode: First call executes immediately, subsequent calls within the interval are debounced.
Previous pending invocations are automatically cancelled.
Exceptions thrown by the action are propagated to the caller.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Debounce.DebounceDispatcher.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified interval.
member.param: The debounce interval in milliseconds. Must be non-negative.
If true, executes on the leading edge (immediately on first call). Default is false (trailing edge).

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Debounce.DebounceDispatcher.#ctor(System.TimeSpan,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified interval.
member.param: The debounce interval as a . Must be non-negative.
If true, executes on the leading edge (immediately on first call). Default is false (trailing edge).

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified interval and time provider.
member.param: The debounce interval in milliseconds. Must be non-negative.
If true, executes on the leading edge (immediately on first call). Default is false (trailing edge).
The time provider to use for delays and time queries.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified interval and time provider.
member.param: The debounce interval as a . Must be non-negative.
If true, executes on the leading edge (immediately on first call). Default is false (trailing edge).
The time provider to use for delays and time queries.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Debounce.DebounceDispatcher.DebounceAsync(System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.Task},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Debounces the execution of an asynchronous action.

remarks.para: In trailing mode (default): Each call cancels any previously pending action and starts a new timer. The action executes only if no new calls occur within the configured interval.
In leading mode: The first call (or first call after the interval expires) executes immediately. Subsequent calls within the interval cancel previous pending actions and are debounced.
Exception Handling: Exceptions thrown by the action are propagated to the caller. Cancellation (either from the token or disposal) is handled silently without throwing exceptions.

member.param: The asynchronous action to invoke after the debounce interval.
Optional cancellation token to cancel the debounced action.
member.returns: A task that completes when the action executes or is cancelled/disposed.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Debounce.DebounceDispatcher.Cancel
member.summary: Cancels any pending debounced action.
member.remarks: This method is thread-safe and can be called concurrently with .

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Debounce.DebounceDispatcher.UpdateIntervalAsync(System.Int32)
member.summary: Updates the debounce interval asynchronously.

remarks.para: This method updates the interval without affecting any currently pending debounced action. The new interval will be used for the next debounce operation.
This method is thread-safe and can be called concurrently with .

member.param: The new debounce interval in milliseconds. Must be non-negative.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Debounce.DebounceDispatcher.UpdateIntervalAsync(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Updates the debounce interval asynchronously.

remarks.para: This method updates the interval without affecting any currently pending debounced action. The new interval will be used for the next debounce operation.
This method is thread-safe and can be called concurrently with .

member.param: The new debounce interval as a . Must be non-negative.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Debounce.DebounceDispatcher.CancelAsync
member.summary: Cancels any pending debounced action asynchronously.
member.remarks: This method is thread-safe and can be called concurrently with .

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Debounce.DebounceDispatcher.Dispose
member.summary: Releases all resources used by the .
member.remarks: This method cancels any pending debounced action and prevents further use of the dispatcher. Cancellation is performed synchronously as this is a synchronous Dispose method.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.MudColor.ToString(System.String,System.IFormatProvider)
member.remarks:
The following formats are available:
rgb Outputs the color in the format "rgb(r,g,b)".
rgba Outputs the color in the format "rgba(r,g,b,a)".
hex Outputs the color in the hexadecimal format "#rrggbb".
hexa Outputs the color in the hexadecimal format with alpha "#rrggbbaa".
colorelements Outputs the color elements without any decorator "r,g,b".

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Utilities.Throttle.ThrottleDispatcher
member.summary: Limits the rate at which an action can be invoked.

remarks.para: This dispatcher implements leading-edge throttling: when called, it executes the action immediately if sufficient time has passed since the last execution started. Subsequent calls within the throttle interval return the same Task as the currently executing action.
Thread Safety: This class is thread-safe. Multiple concurrent calls are properly synchronized.

list.item: The action executes at most once per configured interval.
The first call in a new interval executes immediately (leading edge).
Calls within the interval return the same Task as the executing action.
Exceptions thrown by the action are propagated to all callers within that interval.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Throttle.ThrottleDispatcher.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified interval.
member.param: The minimum interval in milliseconds between invocations. Must be non-negative.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Throttle.ThrottleDispatcher.#ctor(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified interval.
member.param: The minimum interval as a between invocations. Must be non-negative.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Throttle.ThrottleDispatcher.#ctor(System.Int32,System.TimeProvider)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified interval and time provider.
member.param: The minimum interval in milliseconds between invocations. Must be non-negative.
The time provider to use for time queries.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Throttle.ThrottleDispatcher.#ctor(System.TimeSpan,System.TimeProvider)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified interval and time provider.
member.param: The minimum interval as a between invocations. Must be non-negative.
The time provider to use for time queries.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Throttle.ThrottleDispatcher.ThrottleAsync(System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.Task},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Throttles the invocation of an asynchronous action.

remarks.para: If sufficient time has passed since the last execution started, the action executes immediately. Otherwise, this method returns the Task from the currently executing action (same instance).
Exception Handling: Exceptions thrown by the action are propagated to all callers that received the same Task. Cancellation and disposal are handled silently without throwing exceptions.

member.param: The asynchronous action to invoke.
Optional cancellation token. Note: cancellation only prevents new executions; it does not cancel already-running actions.
member.returns: A task representing the action's execution. Multiple calls within the interval return the same task instance, or Task.CompletedTask if cancelled/disposed.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Utilities.Throttle.ThrottleDispatcher.Dispose
member.summary: Releases all resources used by the .
member.remarks: This method prevents further invocations but does not cancel any currently executing action.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudDataGrid`1.CommittedItemChanges
member.summary: Invoked when the user saves changes to an item, allowing for validation, persistence, or other processing.

remarks.para: The callback receives a separate instance of the item with the committed values, preventing changes from being applied to the underlying data source before validation or processing completes.
Return a value to control the edit form behavior. When is :

list.item: - Accepts changes and closes the form
- Rejects changes and keeps the form open for corrections

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudDataGrid`1.RowLoadingContent
member.summary: A RenderFragment that will be used as a placeholder when the Virtualize component is asynchronously loading data. This placeholder is displayed for each item in the data source that is yet to be loaded. Useful for presenting a loading indicator in a data grid row while the actual data is being fetched from the server.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudDataGrid`1.OverscanCount
member.summary: The number of additional items rendered outside the visible region when is true.
member.remarks: Defaults to 3. This value can reduce the amount of rendering during scrolling, but higher values can affect performance.

member.summary: The function which gets data for this grid.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudDataGrid`1.VirtualizeServerData
member.summary: The function which gets data for this grid.
member.remarks: The function accepts a with current sorting, filtering, and pagination parameters. Then, return a with a list of values, and the total (unpaginated) items count in . This property is used when you need to display a list without a paginator, but with loading data from the server as the scroll position changes.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MudExitPrompt
member.summary: Shows a confirmation dialog when the user tries to navigate away.
member.remarks: Due to browser restrictions the native browser exit prompt has to be used on browser navigation.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudExitPrompt.Title
member.summary: The title shown in the confirmation dialog.
member.remarks: Defaults to the localized version of "Confirm navigation".

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudExitPrompt.Text
member.summary: The message shown in the confirmation dialog.
member.remarks: Defaults to the localized version of "Leave site? Changes you made may not be saved.".

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudExitPrompt.Disabled
member.summary: Disables exit prompt protection.
member.remarks: When set to true, navigation proceeds without confirmation and JS unload protection is removed. Defaults to false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudExitPrompt.UseNativePrompt
member.summary: Uses the browser's native confirmation prompt for in-app navigation.
member.remarks: When false, navigation inside the app uses a MudBlazor message box with and . When true, in-app navigation uses the browser confirm dialog and is ignored. Tab close, refresh, and direct URL navigation always use the browser's native unload prompt. Defaults to false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudField.ShrinkLabel
member.summary: Controls whether the label is displayed inside the field or shrinks above it when the field does not have focus.
member.remarks:
Defaults to false.
When false, the label behaves like a placeholder and shrinks only if there is content or an adornment at the start.
When true, the label does not shrink and remains as a placeholder regardless of content, which may result in overlap.

member.remarks: Defaults to null for any file type. Multiple file types must be separated by commas (e.g. ".png,.jpg").

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFileUpload`1.Hidden
member.summary: Hides the internal element.
member.remarks: Defaults to true. When false, the input is visible.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFileUpload`1.DragAndDrop
member.summary: Enables a drag-and-drop area inside the component.
member.remarks: Defaults to false. During drag operations, the internal element can be shown to capture drop events even when is true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFileUpload`1.Dragging
member.summary: Indicates whether a drag operation is active.
member.remarks: When true, the internal element is shown to capture the drop, which temporarily overrides . When false, visibility follows again. Drop-related events reset it to false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFileUpload`1.InputClass
member.summary: The CSS classes applied to the internal element.
member.remarks: Defaults to null. These classes apply when is false. Multiple classes must be separated by spaces.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFileUpload`1.InputStyle
member.summary: The CSS styles applied to the internal element.
member.remarks: Defaults to null. These styles apply when is false. Prefer .

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFileUpload`1.MaximumFileCount
member.summary: The maximum number of files retrieved per selection.
member.remarks: Defaults to 10. This does not limit the total number of files; enforce overall limits in .

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudFileUpload`1.MaxFileSize
member.summary: The maximum allowed file size in bytes.
member.remarks: Defaults to null (no limit). Files exceeding this limit are rejected and a validation error is added.

member.summary: Prevents the user from interacting with this component.
member.remarks: Defaults to false.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudFileUpload`1.GetFilenames
member.summary: Returns the filenames for the selected files.
member.remarks:
When T is , a single filename is returned.
When T is IReadOnlyList<IBrowserFile>, multiple filenames are returned.

member.summary: Removes all files with the specified name from .

member.returns: true if one or more files were removed; otherwise, false if no file with the specified name exists in the current selection.

member.returns: true if the specified file instance was removed; otherwise, false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudForm.Validation
member.summary: The default function or attribute used to validate form components which cannot validate themselves.
member.remarks:
Supported values are:
1. A Func<T,bool> or Func<T,Task<bool>> function. Returns true if valid. When false, a standard "Invalid" message is shown.
2. A Func<T,string> or Func<T,Task<string>> function. Returns null if valid, or a string explaining the error.
3. A Func<T,IEnumerable<string>> or Func<T,Task<IEnumerable<string>>> function. Returns an empty list if valid, or a list of validation errors.
3. A Func<object,string,IEnumerable<string>> or Func<object,string,Task<IEnumerable<string>>> function. Given the form model and path to the member, returns an empty list if valid, or a list of validation errors.
4. A object.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Components.Highlighter.Splitter.HtmlTagMatcher
member.remarks:
Pattern:
(<\\s*/?\\s*\\w+[^>]*?/?>)
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.Compiled | RegexOptions.Singleline
Explanation:
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match '<'.
○ Match a whitespace character greedily any number of times.
○ Match '/' atomically, optionally.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match a word character greedily at least once.
○ Match a character other than '>' lazily any number of times.
○ Match '/' atomically, optionally.
○ Match '>'.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Components.Highlighter.Splitter.HtmlTagParser
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^<\\s*(/)?\\s*(\\w+)[^>]*?(\\/)?\\s*>$
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.Compiled
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match '<'.
○ Match a whitespace character greedily any number of times.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match '/'.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a word character greedily at least once.
○ Match a character other than '>' lazily any number of times.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 3rd capture group.
○ Match '/'.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match '>'.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

Explanation:

Pattern:
^.$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match any character other than '\n'.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.RegisterChild(MudBlazor.MudMenu)
member.summary: Registers a child menu with this menu, allowing for hierarchical menu management. This is crucial for controlling the open/close state of nested menus.
member.param: The child to register.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.UnregisterChild(MudBlazor.MudMenu)
member.summary: Unregisters a child menu from this menu. This is called when a child menu is disposed or removed, maintaining accurate tracking of nested menus.
member.param: The child to unregister.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.CloseMenuAsync
member.summary: Closes this menu and any descendants if it's a nested menu.
member.remarks: It ensures that all nested menus are also closed when a parent menu is closed.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.CloseAllMenusAsync
member.summary: Closes all menus in the hierarchy, starting from the top-most parent.
member.remarks: This is useful for dismissing all open menus with a single action, such as clicking outside the menu area.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.OpenMenuAsync(System.EventArgs,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Opens the menu or updates its state if it's already open.

param.name: args
param.para: The event arguments for the activation event; or .
When is true, the menu will be positioned at these coordinates.

member.param: If true, the menu will close automatically when the pointer leaves its bounds.
member.remarks: Parents are not automatically opened when a child is opened.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.OpenSubMenuAsync(System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Closes sibling menus before opening as a "mouse over" menu. It prevents multiple sub-menus at the same level from being open simultaneously when hovering.
member.remarks: This is called in place of if the menu activator is implicitly rendered for the submenu.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.ToggleMenuAsync(System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Toggles the menu's open or closed state.

param.name: args
param.para: The event arguments for the activation event; or .
When is true, the menu will be positioned at these coordinates.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.IsHoverable(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.PointerEventArgs)
member.summary: Determines if the menu should respond to hover events.
member.remarks: This prevents hover-related actions on devices that don't support traditional hovering (e.g., touchscreens).

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.PointerEnterAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.PointerEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles the pointer entering either the activator or the menu list.
member.remarks: This initiates a hover delay before opening the menu to provide a more forgiving user experience.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.PointerLeaveAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.PointerEventArgs)
member.summary: Handles the pointer leaving either the activator or the menu list.
member.remarks: This introduces a delay before closing the menu to allow smooth transitions between nested menus.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.HasPointerOver(MudBlazor.MudMenu)
member.summary: Recursively checks if the pointer is currently over this menu or any of its sub-menus.
member.remarks: This is crucial for determining when to close hover-activated menus.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudMenu.CancelPendingActions
member.summary: Cancels any pending hover or leave actions.
member.remarks: This is called when a new menu action is initiated, preventing conflicting or stale operations.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MudPagination
member.summary: A list of clickable page numbers along with navigation buttons.

member.summary: Displays text right-to-left.

member.summary: The total number of pages.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudPagination.BoundaryCount
member.summary: The number of pages shown before and after the ellipsis.
member.remarks:
Defaults to 1.
A value of 1 would show one-page number at the edge: < 1 ... 4 5 6 ... 9 >
A value of 2 would show two-page numbers at the edge: < 1 2 ... 4 5 6 ... 8 9 >

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudPagination.MiddleCount
member.summary: The number of pages shown between the ellipsis.
member.remarks:
Defaults to 1.
A value of 1 would show one-page number in the middle: < 1 ... 5 ... 9 >
A value of 3 would show three-page numbers in the middle: < 1 ... 4 5 6 ... 9 >

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudSelectItem`1.OnMudShadowSelectChanged(MudBlazor.State.ParameterChangedEventArgs{MudBlazor.IMudShadowSelect})
member.summary: Handles changes to the IMudShadowSelect cascading parameter.
member.remarks: This is invoked when the shadow select parent changes (e.g., when moving between different selects). It unregisters from the old parent and registers with the new one.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudSelectItem`1.OnMudSelectChanged(MudBlazor.State.ParameterChangedEventArgs{MudBlazor.IMudSelect})
member.summary: Handles changes to the IMudSelect cascading parameter.

remarks.para: This is invoked when the select parent changes (e.g., when moving between different selects). It handles the complete lifecycle:

item.description: Unsubscribes from the old parent's selection changes
Unregisters from the old parent's context
Registers with the new parent's context
Subscribes to the new parent's selection changes

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudSelectItem`1.OnSelectionChangedAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyCollection{`0})
member.summary: Handles selection changes from the parent select.
member.remarks: This callback is invoked when the parent's SelectedValues changes. It updates the local Selected state and triggers a re-render if needed. This replaces the OnUpdateSelectionStateFromOutside method.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MudStack
member.summary: Manages layout of its child items along the vertical or horizontal axis with optional spacing.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudStack.getFlexDirection
member.summary: Gets the CSS flex direction based on the current breakpoint and row settings.
member.remarks: This property generates a CSS flex direction string based on the following conditions: - If the breakpoint is not set or is "none" or "always", it returns the default direction based on the Row and Reverse properties. - If the breakpoint is one of the predefined sizes ("xs", "sm", "md", "lg", "xl", "xxl"), it generates a flex direction string that changes at the specified breakpoint. - If the breakpoint ends with "anddown", it generates a flex direction string that applies up to and including the specified breakpoint. - If the breakpoint ends with "andup", it generates a flex direction string that applies from the specified breakpoint and up.
member.returns: A string representing the CSS flex direction based on the current breakpoint and row settings.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudStack.Row
member.summary: Displays items horizontally.
member.remarks: Defaults to false. When true, items will be displayed horizontally. When false, items are displayed vertically.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudStack.Reverse
member.summary: Reverses the order of items.
member.remarks: Defaults to false. When true, items will be reversed.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudStack.Spacing
member.summary: The gap between items in increments of 4px.

remarks.para: Defaults to 3.
Maximum is 20 (80px).

member.summary: The columns for each row in this table.
member.remarks: Use to define columns, and context to access item properties for each column.

member.summary: The optional nested content underneath each row.
member.remarks: Use and to define the child content, and context to access item properties.

member.summary: The columns for each row when a row is being edited.
member.remarks: Use to define columns, and context to access item properties for each column. Typically looks similar to rows in but with edit components.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTable`1.RowEditableFunc
member.summary: The function which determines if a row can be edited.
member.remarks: Make the function return true to allow editing, and false to prevent it. When no value is set, all rows are considered editable.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTable`1.RowDisabledFunc
member.summary: The function which determines if a row is disabled. A disabled row ignores mouse events and uses --mud-palette-text-disabled as color.
member.remarks: Make the function return true to diable the corresponding row. When no value is set, all rows are considered enabled.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTable`1.DisabledRowClass
member.summary: The class to use for disabled rows.
member.remarks: Defaults to mud-table-row-disabled

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTable`1.Columns
member.summary: The columns for each row in this table.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTable`1.NoRecordsContent
member.summary: The content shown when there are no rows to display.
member.remarks: No or is necessary.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTable`1.LoadingContent
member.summary: The content shown while Loading is true and the table has no rows.
member.remarks:
Rendered as a single table row containing one cell that spans the table width. Use this for simple messages or placeholders (for example "Loading…").
This content is displayed in addition to the table's loading animation.
For multi-row or multi-column loading layouts, use instead.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTable`1.LoadingContentBody
member.summary: The content shown while Loading is true and the table has no rows.
member.remarks:
Rendered directly into the table body at the top. You must supply valid table row/cell markup (for example <MudTr> and <MudTd>). If you place plain text or non-table markup here most browsers will ignore it. Use this when you need to produce arbitrary rows and columns while the table is loading.
This content is displayed in addition to the table's loading animation.
For a single row column loading layout or text, use instead.
This value is optional, and will override if not null.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTable`1.HorizontalScrollbar
member.summary: Shows a horizontal scroll bar if the content exceeds the maximum width.
member.remarks: Defaults to false. Typically true for tables with more columns than can fit on the screen.

member.summary: The data to display.
member.remarks: When set, should not be set. Use that property to get data from a back end.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.AriaLabel
member.summary: The aria-label for the HTML table element.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.IsEditRowSwitchingBlocked
member.summary: Forces a row being edited to be saved or canceled before a new row can be selected.
member.remarks: Defaults to false. When false, a new row can be edited even if the previous one has not been saved or canceled.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.Elevation
member.summary: The size of the drop shadow.
member.remarks: Defaults to 1. A higher number creates a heavier drop shadow. Use a value of 0 for no shadow.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.Square
member.summary: Uses square corners for the table.
member.remarks: Defaults to false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.Outlined
member.summary: Shows borders around the table.
member.remarks: Defaults to true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.Bordered
member.summary: Shows left and right borders for each table cell.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.Dense
member.summary: Uses compact padding for all rows.
member.remarks: Defaults to false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.CellClass
member.summary: The CSS classes applied to all cells of the table.
member.remarks: Multiple classes must be separated by spaces.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.Hover
member.summary: Highlights rows when hovering over them.
member.remarks: Defaults to false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.Striped
member.summary: Uses alternating colors for table rows.
member.remarks: Defaults to false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.Breakpoint
member.summary: The screen width at which this table switches to small-device mode.
member.remarks: Defaults to . Allowed values are None, Xs, Sm, Md, Lg, and Xl.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.FixedHeader
member.summary: Fixes the table header in place while the table is scrolled.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.FixedFooter

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTableBase.Height

member.remarks: If inclusive is true, it returns the width or height of the panel item selected as well. For horizontal tabs, this is the width; for vertical tabs, this is the height.

member.summary: Controls which values can be edited.

member.summary: The amount of time, in milliseconds, to wait before closing the picker.
member.remarks: Defaults to 200. The delay gives users a moment to see the selected time before the popover disappears.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTimePicker.AutoClose
member.summary: Closes this picker when the value is set or cleared.
member.remarks: Defaults to false. When true and PickerActions are defined, the hour and the minutes can be selected and the drop-down will close without having to click any of the action buttons.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTimePicker.MinuteSelectionStep
member.summary: The step interval when selecting minutes.
member.remarks: Defaults to 1. For example: a value of 15 would allow minutes 0, 15, 30, and 45 be selected.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTimePicker.AmPm
member.summary: Shows a 12-hour selection clock.
member.remarks:
Defaults to false.
When true, hours 1-12 are displayed with an AM or PM marker.
When false, hours 0-23 are displayed.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudTimePicker.TimeFormat
member.summary: The format applied to time values.
member.remarks:
Defaults to hh:mm tt when is true, otherwise HH:mm.
Format strings are typically a combination of these characters:
* h (lowercase) for hours in 12-hour time,
* H (uppercase) for hours in 24-hour time,
* m for minutes,
* tt for AM/PM markers.
For example: h:mm tt would display 6:32 PM, and HH:mm would display 18:32.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudVirtualize`1.OverscanCount
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that determines how many additional items will be rendered before and after the visible region. This help to reduce the frequency of rendering during scrolling. However, higher values mean that more elements will be present in the page.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudVirtualize`1.ItemSize
member.summary: Gets the size of each item in pixels. Defaults to 50px.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudVirtualize`1.MaxItemCount
member.summary:
Gets or sets the maximum number of items that will be rendered, even if the client reports that its viewport is large enough to show more. The default value is 100.
This should only be used as a safeguard against excessive memory usage or large data loads. Do not set this to a smaller number than you expect to fit on a realistic-sized window, because that will leave a blank gap below and the user may not be able to see the rest of the content.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudVirtualize`1.SpacerElement
member.summary: Gets or sets tag name of the HTML element that will be used as virtualization spacer. Default is div.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MudVirtualize`1.RefreshDataAsync
member.summary: Refreshes the data in the Virtualize component asynchronously.

member.summary: Default reversible converter that converts between and .

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of and registers the built-in converters with a reversible dispatcher.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.DeferredConverter`2
member.summary: A simple reversible converter whose forward and backward delegates can be supplied (deferred) after construction.
member.remarks: This type is useful to break initialization cycles or to construct converter graphs where delegates are wired at a later time. The converter will throw if a conversion method is invoked before the corresponding delegate has been set.
member.typeparam: The input type for the forward conversion.
The output type for the forward conversion (and the input type for the backward conversion).

member.summary: Sets the forward conversion delegate.
member.param: A delegate that converts a value to .
member.remarks: Calling this method replaces any previously registered forward delegate.

member.summary: Sets the backward conversion delegate.

member.remarks: Use when no conversion is required but an instance is needed (for example as a default or placeholder converter). Both and simply return the supplied value.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.IConverter`2
member.summary: Converts values from to .
member.typeparam: The input type. Declared contravariant (in) so implementations can accept base types.
The output type. Declared covariant (out) so implementations can return derived types.
member.remarks: Implementations should perform a synchronous conversion. Converters are typically used by UI components and utilities; prefer pure, side-effect-free implementations.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.IConverter`2.Convert(`0)
member.summary: Converts the specified value to the target type.
member.param: The value to convert.
member.returns: The converted value as .

member.name: T:MudBlazor.ICultureAwareConverter
member.summary: Provides culture and format providers for converters that need culture-aware formatting or parsing.
member.remarks:
This interface is intended primarily for converters that are used by components via . When a converter implementing this interface is supplied to a Mud form component, the component will automatically provide the and delegates at runtime.
If you plan to use a converter outside of Blazor components (for example in plain services or library code), prefer not to implement this interface. Instead, provide culture/format information explicitly to the converter (for example via constructor parameters or required properties)
Both members are delegates so callers can supply dynamic providers (for example a component-local culture or a runtime format string). Implementations should invoke these delegates at conversion time to obtain the current and format string. When returns null, the implementation should use the default formatting behavior.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.ICultureAwareConverter.Culture
member.summary: A function that returns the to use when converting or formatting values.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.ICultureAwareConverter.Format
member.summary: A function that provides an optional format string to use when formatting values.
member.remarks: The delegate is invoked at conversion/formatting time and may return null to indicate that the default formatting rules should be applied. Typical uses include custom date/time or numeric format strings supplied by the consumer. When the converter is used as a component converter (via ), the host component will supply this delegate automatically.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.IReversibleConverter`2
member.summary: Extends with the ability to convert values back from to .
member.typeparam: The input type for the forward conversion and the result type for the reverse conversion.
The output type for the forward conversion and the input type for the reverse conversion.
member.remarks: Implementations should provide a best-effort inverse for . Reversibility is not guaranteed for all converters (for example when information is lost during the forward conversion); implementations may throw an if the reverse conversion cannot be performed. Prefer pure, side-effect-free implementations consistent with semantics.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.IReversibleConverter`2.ConvertBack(`1)
member.summary: Converts the specified value from back to .
member.param: The value to convert back.
member.returns: The converted value as .
member.exception: Thrown when the conversion cannot be performed or is not supported for the provided value.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.RangeConverter`1
member.summary: Converter that serializes and deserializes a to and from a string representation.
member.typeparam: The element type of the range.
member.remarks: The string representation is in the form [{start};{end}]. Empty or null ranges are represented as an empty string. This converter delegates element-level conversions to a instance and implements so components (for example via MudFormComponent.Converter) can inject the and providers automatically.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.RangeConverter`1.Format
member.summary: A function that provides the optional format string used by inner converters. When used as a component converter the host will supply this delegate automatically.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.RangeConverter`1.Culture
member.summary: A function that returns the used by inner converters. When used as a component converter the host will supply this delegate automatically.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.RangeConverter`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of and configures the inner element converter.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Edge
member.summary: Specifies where a negative margin is applied within a layout component. This is typically used in conjunction with elements that have leading or trailing adornments.
member.remarks: Negative margins are often applied to ensure adornments or icons align more closely with the edge of the component. Depending on the design requirements, this margin can be applied at the start or end of an element, or not applied at all (default behavior).

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Edge.False
member.summary: No negative margin is applied to the element.
member.remarks: This is the default behavior, meaning the element maintains its normal margins without adjustments. It is used when there is no need to modify the element’s margin for adornments or icons.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Edge.Start
member.summary: A negative margin is applied to the start of the element.
member.remarks: This reduces the space at the start (leading side) of the element. It is typically used when an adornment, such as an icon or label, is positioned at the beginning of the component and needs to be aligned more closely with the left edge in left-to-right layouts (or the right edge in right-to-left layouts).

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Edge.End
member.summary: A negative margin is applied to the end of the element.
member.remarks: This reduces the space at the end (trailing side) of the element. It is commonly used when an adornment, such as an icon or label, is positioned at the end of the component and requires alignment with the right edge in left-to-right layouts (or the left edge in right-to-left layouts).

member.name: T:MudBlazor.HorizontalAlignment

member.summary: The component is horizontally centered.

member.summary: The component is aligned to the left.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.HorizontalAlignment.Right
member.summary: The component is aligned to the right.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.HorizontalAlignment.Start
member.summary: The component is aligned based on Right-to-Left (RTL) settings.
member.remarks: When Right-to-Left is enabled, the component is aligned to the right. Otherwise, the left.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.HorizontalAlignment.End
member.summary: The component is aligned based on Right-to-Left (RTL) settings.
member.remarks: When Right-to-Left is enabled, the component is aligned to the left. Otherwise, the right.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.InputMode
member.summary: The inputmode global attribute is an enumerated attribute that hints at the type of data that might be entered by the user while editing the element or its contents. Not supported by safari. Use Pattern to achieve special mobile keyboards in safari. https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/inputmode

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputMode.none
member.summary: No virtual keyboard. For when the page implements its own keyboard input control. Reference

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputMode.text
member.summary: Default. Standard input keyboard for the user's current locale. Reference

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputMode.decimal
member.summary: Fractional numeric input keyboard containing the digits and decimal separator for the user's locale (typically . or ,). Devices may or may not show a minus key (-). Reference

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputMode.numeric
member.summary: Numeric input keyboard, but only requires the digits 0–9. Devices may or may not show a minus key. Reference

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputMode.tel
member.summary: A telephone keypad input, including the digits 0–9, the asterisk (*), and the pound (#) key. Inputs that require a telephone number should typically use <input type="tel">instead. Reference

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputMode.search
member.summary: A virtual keyboard optimized for search input. For instance, the return/submit key may be labeled “Search”, along with possible other optimizations. Inputs that require a search query should typically use <input type="search"> instead. Reference

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputMode.email
member.summary: A virtual keyboard optimized for entering email addresses. Typically includes the @ character as well as other optimizations. Inputs that require email addresses should typically use <input type="email"> instead. Reference

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputMode.url
member.summary: A keypad optimized for entering URLs. This may have the / key more prominent, for example. Enhanced features could include history access and so on. Inputs that require a URL should typically use <input type="url"> instead. Reference

member.name: T:MudBlazor.InputSizing
member.summary: The strategy for how the input calculates its height.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputSizing.Fixed
member.summary: The height is fixed based on the Lines property.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.InputSizing.Auto
member.summary: The height grows and shrinks dynamically to fit the text content. Uses Lines as minimum and MaxLines as maximum.
member.remarks: Previously known as "AutoGrow".

member.summary: Indicates how an object is sized relative to its container.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ObjectFit.None
member.summary: The image is cropped to fit within its container.

member.summary: The content is sized to maintain its aspect ratio while filling the container.
member.remarks: If the object's aspect ratio does not match the aspect ratio of its container, then the object will be clipped to fit.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ObjectFit.Contain
member.summary: The content is scaled to maintain its aspect ratio while filling the container.
member.remarks: The object is scaled to fill the container, while preserving its aspect ratio, which may cause a "letterbox" if its aspect ratio does not match the aspect ratio of its container.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ObjectFit.Fill
member.summary: The content is sized to fill the container.
member.remarks: The object will completely fill the container. If the object's aspect ratio does not match the aspect ratio of its container, then the object will be stretched to fit.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.ObjectFit.ScaleDown
member.summary: The content is sized as if or were specified, whichever would result in a smaller object size.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Origin.BottomRight

member.name: T:MudBlazor.OverflowBehavior
member.summary: Controls how a component behaves when the browser is scrolled.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.OverflowBehavior.FlipNever
member.summary: Prevents any adjustment of the component, even if it would overflow the container.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.OverflowBehavior.FlipOnOpen
member.summary: Flips the component if it would overflow its container, but only when it first opens. Does not update dynamically if overflow changes afterwards.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.OverflowBehavior.FlipAlways
member.summary: Flips the component if it would overflow its container, dynamically adjusting as necessary to prevent overflow.
member.remarks: This is the default for popovers unless otherwise set.

member.summary: Items alternate on either side of centered dots.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.TimelinePosition.Top
member.summary: Dots are displayed above the text of each timeline item.
member.remarks: Only applies if is .

member.name: F:MudBlazor.TimelinePosition.Bottom
member.summary: Dots are displayed below the text of each timeline item.
member.remarks: Only applies if is .

member.name: F:MudBlazor.TimelinePosition.Left
member.summary: Dots are displayed to the left of text for each timeline item.
member.remarks: Only applies if is .

member.name: F:MudBlazor.TimelinePosition.Right
member.summary: Dots are displayed to the right of text for each timeline item.
member.remarks: Only applies if is .

member.name: F:MudBlazor.TimelinePosition.Start
member.summary: Dots are displayed at the start based on the Right-to-Left setting of the .
member.remarks:
Only applies if is .
When Right-to-Left is enabled, the dots are displayed on the right of each item's text.
When Right-to-Left is disabled, the dots are displayed on the left of each item's text.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.TimelinePosition.End
member.summary: Dots are displayed at the end based on the Right-to-Left setting of the .
member.remarks:
Only applies if is .
When Right-to-Left is enabled, the dots are displayed on the left of each item's text.
When Right-to-Left is disabled, the dots are displayed on the right of each item's text.
