member.name: M:MudBlazor.Extensions.EnumerableExtensions.AsReadOnlyCollection``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Converts an into an with minimal overhead.
member.remarks:
This method attempts to avoid unnecessary allocations by using fast paths:
If already implements , it is returned directly.
If implements , it is wrapped in a lightweight read-only adapter without copying the underlying data.
If is null, an empty read-only collection is returned.
Otherwise, the sequence is enumerated once and materialized into a .
This ensures callers always receive an while avoiding unnecessary enumeration or allocation whenever possible.
member.param: The sequence to convert to a read-only collection.
member.typeparam: The element type.
member.returns: An representing the contents of .

member.returns: An array that contains the values of constant in type

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Services.ResizeOptions
member.summary: Options that control browser resize observation and breakpoint handling.
member.remarks: Configure these values when subscribing to the viewport service to balance responsiveness, logging, and breakpoint behavior for your component.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Services.ResizeOptions.ReportRate
member.summary: Rate in milliseconds that the browsers `resize()` event should report a change. Setting this value too low can cause poor application performance. Default value is 100.
member.remarks: If set to 0, the resize event will report instantaneously.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Services.ResizeOptions.EnableLogging
member.summary: Report resize events and media queries in the browser's console. Default value is false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Services.ResizeOptions.SuppressInitEvent
member.summary: Suppress the first OnResized that is invoked when a new event handler is added. Default value is true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Services.ResizeOptions.NotifyOnBreakpointOnly
member.summary: If true, RaiseOnResized is called only when breakpoint has changed. Default value is true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.Services.ResizeOptions.BreakpointDefinitions
member.summary: Gets or sets the breakpoint definitions, representing specific breakpoints and their associated width.
member.value: A dictionary where each entry represents a breakpoint, and the corresponding int value represents the width.
member.remarks: When the dictionary is null or empty, the default breakpoint definitions will be used. The default breakpoint definitions are as follows: [Breakpoint.Xxl] = 2560, [Breakpoint.Xl] = 1920, [Breakpoint.Lg] = 1280, [Breakpoint.Md] = 960, [Breakpoint.Sm] = 600, [Breakpoint.Xs] = 0.

member.summary: Maps multiple keys to an action on key down with a shared condition. More efficient than calling OnKeyDown multiple times with the same condition.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Services.KeyMapBuilder.When(System.Func{System.Boolean},System.Action{MudBlazor.Services.KeyMapBuilder})
member.summary: Creates a conditional scope where all commands share the same condition. This is more efficient than adding the condition to each command individually. Hooks within the scope are still inserted at the beginning to ensure they execute first.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Services.KeyMapBuilder.HookKeyDown(System.Func{Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.KeyboardEventArgs,System.Threading.Tasks.Task})
member.summary: Registers a hook that will be called for every key down event, allowing subsequent commands to also execute. Unlike regular commands which stop the command chain after execution, hooks do not stop the chain. This is useful for maintaining virtual method override patterns while using the KeyCommand API.

remarks.para: Important: Hooks are always executed before regular commands, regardless of their declaration order. You can declare hooks anywhere in the builder chain, and they will be automatically moved to execute first.
remarks.code: await KeyInterceptorService.SubscribeAsync(elementId, options, keys => keys .OnKeyDown("Backspace", HandleBackspaceAsync) // Regular commands .HookKeyDown(OnHandleKeyDownAsync) // Hook still executes first for ALL keys .When(CanHandleKeys, builder => builder .OnKeyDownAny(["Escape", "Tab"], CloseAsync)));
remarks.para: The hook receives the KeyboardEventArgs and can perform any necessary processing (e.g., calling a virtual method that derived classes can override). The hook will execute for every key down event, regardless of whether any specific key commands match.
If a hook is placed inside a When() scope, it will still respect the condition - it won't execute unconditionally.

member.param: The method to call on every key down event.
member.returns: The builder for chaining.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Services.KeyMapBuilder.HookKeyUp(System.Func{Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.KeyboardEventArgs,System.Threading.Tasks.Task})
member.summary: Registers a hook that will be called for every key up event, allowing subsequent commands to also execute. Unlike regular commands which stop the command chain after execution, hooks do not stop the chain. This is useful for maintaining virtual method override patterns while using the KeyCommand API.

remarks.para: Important: Hooks are always executed before regular commands, regardless of their declaration order. You can declare hooks anywhere in the builder chain, and they will be automatically moved to execute first.
remarks.code: await KeyInterceptorService.SubscribeAsync(elementId, options, keys => keys .OnKeyUp("Enter", HandleEnterAsync) // Regular commands .HookKeyUp(OnHandleKeyUpAsync) // Hook still executes first for ALL keys .When(CanHandleKeys, builder => builder .OnKeyUpAny(["Escape", "Tab"], CloseAsync)));
remarks.para: The hook receives the KeyboardEventArgs and can perform any necessary processing (e.g., calling a virtual method that derived classes can override). The hook will execute for every key up event, regardless of whether any specific key commands match.
If a hook is placed inside a When() scope, it will still respect the condition - it won't execute unconditionally.

member.param: The method to call on every key up event.
member.returns: The builder for chaining.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Services.KeyOptions
member.summary: Configuration for how a specific key should be intercepted, including preventDefault/stopPropagation expressions and which C# callbacks should fire.

remarks.para: For PreventDown, PreventUp, StopDown and StopUp the configuration which key combinations should match is a JavaScript boolean expression.
Examples: For the examples, let's assume the Tab key was pressed. Note: for combinations of more than one modifier the following order of modifiers must be followed strictly: shift+ctrl+alt+meta

* Don't prevent key down: PreventDown=null or PreventDown="none" * Prevent key down of unmodified keystrokes such as "Tab": PreventDown="key+none" * Prevent key down of Tab and Ctrl+Tab PreventDown="key+none|key+ctrl" * Prevent key down of just Ctrl+Tab PreventDown="key+ctrl" * Prevent key down of Ctrl+Tab and Shift+Tab but not Shift+Ctrl+Tab: PreventDown="key+shift|key+ctrl" * Prevent key down of Shift+Ctrl+Tab and Ctrl+Tab but not Shift+Tab: PreventDown="key+shift+ctrl|key+ctrl" * Prevent any combination of key and modifiers, but not the unmodified key: PreventDown="key+any" * Prevent any combination of key and modifiers, even the unmodified key: PreventDown="any"
Use this when you need fine-grained control over browser key behavior and want to keep the configuration declarative instead of ad-hoc JS interop.

summary.para: Examples: " " for space, "Tab" for tab, "a" for lowercase A-key. Also allowed: JS regex such as "/[a-z]/" or "/a|b/" but NOT "/[a-z]/g" or "/[a-z]/i" regex must be enclosed in two forward slashes!

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Services.ResizeObserver

member.remarks: This service bridges Blazor components to the browser ResizeObserver API through JS interop. Components use it to subscribe once and then query cached sizes during rendering without extra JS roundtrips.

member.summary: Payload sent from JavaScript to describe which element changed and its latest bounds.

The new size of the element.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Breakpoint
member.summary: Indicates a browser width used to trigger behaviors.
member.remarks: Breakpoints are typically used to show or hide content based on the width of the browser window, such as customizing content for desktops, tablets, and mobile devices.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.Xs
member.summary: A small to large phone.
member.remarks: 600 pixels wide or less.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.Sm
member.summary: A small to medium tablet.
member.remarks: Between 600 and 960 pixels wide.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.Md
member.summary: A large tablet or laptop.
member.remarks: Between 960 and 1280 pixels wide.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.Lg
member.summary: A desktop computer.
member.remarks: Between 1280 and 1920 pixels wide.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.Xl
member.summary: A high-definition or 4K desktop computer monitor.
member.remarks: Between 1920 and 2560 pixels wide.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.Xxl
member.summary: An ultra-wide of 4K+ desktop computer monitor.
member.remarks: 2560 or more pixels wide.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.SmAndDown
member.summary: A small to medium tablet, or smaller device.
member.remarks: 960 pixels wide, or less.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.MdAndDown
member.summary: A large tablet, laptop, tablet, or smaller device.
member.remarks: 1280 pixels wide, or less.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.LgAndDown
member.summary: A desktop computer or smaller device.
member.remarks: 1920 pixels wide, or less.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.XlAndDown

member.remarks: 2560 pixels wide, or less.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.MdAndUp

member.remarks: 960 pixels wide, or more.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.Breakpoint.XlAndUp

Indicates whether the event will fire immediately with the current and information without waiting for changes. When set to true, the event will be fired immediately. When set to false, it will wait for the service to observe any changes before firing the event. The event won't fire if such already exist, for example when you re-subscribe.

The list of arguments to be passed to the string resource
member.returns: The string resource as a .
member.remarks: This method is called when the default English translation is ignored or unavailable, and a custom MudLocalizer service implementation is registered. member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudGlobal.UnhandledExceptionHandler
member.summary: The handler for unhandled MudBlazor component exceptions.
member.remarks:
Exceptions which use this handler are typically rare, such as errors which occur during a "fire-and-forget" which cannot be awaited.
By default, exceptions are logged to the console via .
To handle all .NET exceptions, see: Handle errors in ASP.NET Core.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.PointerEventsNoneOptions
member.summary: Options for configuring the pointer-events-none interop service.
member.remarks: Use these options when you need to disable pointer events on elements while still tracking pointer up/down notifications for cleanup or state transitions.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PointerEventsNoneOptions.EnableLogging
member.summary: Output event and debug information to the browser's console.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PointerEventsNoneOptions.SubscribeDown
member.summary: Subscribe to pointer down events.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PointerEventsNoneOptions.SubscribeUp
member.summary: Subscribe to pointer up events.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.PointerEventsNoneService
member.summary: Globally observes pointer events for elements that have pointer events disabled.
member.remarks:
This implementation uses JavaScript interop to globally listen for pointer events such as pointer down and up. Since elements with pointer-events: none do not receive events normally, the interop captures these events and checks if they occurred over any registered element IDs. Matching observers are then notified in C#.
This allows you to make elements with disabled pointer interaction still participate in interaction logic, such as overlays or custom render layers.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PointerEventsNoneService.ObserversCount
member.summary: Gets the number of observers.
member.remarks: This property is not exposed in the public API of the interface and is intended for internal use only.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.PointerEventsNoneService.RaiseOnPointerDown(System.String[])
member.summary: Notifies observers when a pointer down event occurs over one or more of the specified HTML elements. This method is invoked from JavaScript via interop.
member.param: An array of element IDs for which the pointer down event was detected.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous notification operation.
member.remarks: This method is not exposed in the public API of the interface and is intended for internal use only.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.PointerEventsNoneService.RaiseOnPointerUp(System.String[])
member.summary: Notifies observers when a pointer up event occurs on any of the specified HTML elements. This method is invoked from JavaScript via interop.
member.param: An array of HTML element IDs that received the pointer up event.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous notification operation.
member.remarks: This method is not exposed in the public API of the interface and is intended for internal use only. It is called by the JavaScript layer when a pointer up event is detected globally over elements with pointer-events: none.

member.remarks: This property is used to determine the detachment state of the popover on the JavaScript side. It indicates whether the popover is detached from its parent component, allowing it to be rendered outside the normal component hierarchy.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions
member.summary: Options that control how MudBlazor popovers are positioned, animated, and managed.
member.remarks: Configure this when registering MudBlazor services to keep popover behavior consistent across selects, menus, tooltips, and other popover-based components.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.Delay
member.summary: Gets or sets the amount of time in milliseconds to wait from opening the popover before beginning to perform the transition. The default value is 0.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.Duration
member.summary: Gets or sets the length of time that the opening transition takes to complete. The default value is 251 milliseconds.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.CheckForPopoverProvider
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to check for the presence of a popover provider . The default value is true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.ContainerClass
member.summary: Gets or sets the CSS class of the popover container. The default value is mud-popover-provider.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.FlipMargin
member.summary: Gets or sets the FlipMargin for the popover. The default value is 0.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.QueueDelay
member.summary: Gets the delay for batch popovers detachment. The default value is 0.5 seconds.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.OverflowPadding
member.summary: Gets or sets the overflow padding for the popover. This is used when adjusting popovers that go off screen at the top or left. It is also used to create max-height for popovers containing a list that will go off screen. The default value is 24 roughly equal to the 8dp margin of material design.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.ThrowOnDuplicateProvider
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to throw an exception when a duplicate is encountered. The default value is true.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.ModalOverlay
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the modal overlay prevents interaction with background elements.
member.remarks: Only applies to components that use a in conjunction with a to close the popover when a user clicks outside, such as . The default value is false.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PopoverOptions.OverflowBehavior
member.summary: Gets or sets the behavior applied when there is not enough space for a dropdown popover to be visible.
member.remarks: The default value is .

member.name: T:MudBlazor.PopoverService
member.summary: Manages popover lifecycles, state updates, and JS positioning for all active popovers.
member.remarks: This service is the backbone for menu, select, tooltip, and other popover-based components. It centralizes creation, updates, and disposal while coordinating with popover providers.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.IScrollManager.ScrollToListItemAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Scrolls to the list item element with the given ID.
member.param: The ID of the list item element to scroll.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.IScrollManager.LockScrollAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Locks the scroll for the element matching the specified selector by adding a CSS class.
member.param: The CSS selector of the element to lock scroll. Defaults to "body".
The CSS class to add to lock scroll. Defaults to "scroll-locked".
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.IScrollManager.UnlockScrollAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Unlocks the scroll for the element matching the specified selector by removing a CSS class.
member.param: The CSS selector of the element to unlock scroll. Defaults to "body".
The CSS class to remove to unlock scroll. Defaults to "scroll-locked".
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.IScrollManager.ScrollToBottomAsync(System.String,MudBlazor.ScrollBehavior)
member.summary: Scrolls to the bottom of the element with the given ID.
member.param: The ID of the element to scroll.
The scroll behavior (e.g., smooth or auto). Defaults to auto.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.IScrollManager.ScrollToVirtualizedItemAsync(System.String,System.Int32,System.Double,System.String,MudBlazor.ScrollBehavior)
member.summary: Scrolls a virtualized container to bring a specific item into view. It first estimates the scroll position based on item index and height, then attempts to adjust the scroll to ensure the specific item (by its ID) is as close to the 'nearest' edge as possible.
member.param: The ID of the scrollable HTML element that contains the virtualized items.
The index of the item to scroll to within the virtualized list.
The estimated height of a single item in pixels. Used for the initial scroll positioning.
The complete ID of the specific target HTML element (e.g., row) to bring into view. This allows for a precise final scroll adjustment after the initial estimated scroll.
The desired scroll behavior (e.g., smooth, auto).
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.CollectionComparer`1
member.summary:
Provides a comparer for values using a . Equality is set-based: two collections are equal if they contain the same distinct elements, regardless of order or the number of duplicates. Null is only equal to null.
Note:
The order of elements does not affect equality or hash code.
Multiple entries of the same value are ignored.
Null and empty collections are treated as distinct values.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.EventUtil
member.summary: Utility class for opting out of rerendering in Blazor when an EventCallback is invoked. By default, components inherit from ComponentBase, which automatically invokes StateHasChanged after the component's event handlers are invoked. In some cases, it might be unnecessary or undesirable to trigger a rerender after an event handler is invoked. For example, an event handler might not modify the component state. https://learn.microsoft.com/aspnet/core/blazor/performance?view=aspnetcore-6.0#avoid-rerendering-after-handling-events-without-state-changes

The action callback to be converted.
member.returns: A non-rendering event handler.

member.returns: A non-rendering event handler.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Identifier
member.summary: Provides high-performance methods to create unique identifiers with optional prefixes.

remarks.para: This class uses optimized algorithms to generate identifiers quickly while maintaining uniqueness. Identifiers consist of lowercase letters and digits from the set [a-z0-9].

Performance is prioritized over perfect uniform distribution. The implementation uses modulo operations, which introduce a slight modulo bias so that some characters may appear marginally more frequently than others. For the purposes of identifier generation, this bias is negligible.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Identifier.Create(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a unique identifier with the specified prefix.
member.param: The prefix to prepend to the unique identifier.
member.returns: A unique identifier string with the specified prefix.

example.code: var id = Identifier.Create("button"); // Returns something like: "buttonx7k2n9q4"

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Identifier.Create
member.summary: Creates a unique identifier with a randomly generated prefix.
member.returns: A unique identifier string.
member.remarks: Unlike a fixed prefix, this method generates a random first character for improved uniqueness.

example.code: var id = Identifier.Create(); // Returns something like: "m7k2n9q4p" (9 characters, first one random)

member.name: M:MudBlazor.Identifier.GetCharFromRandomBits(System.Int64,System.Int32)
member.summary: Extracts a character from random bits using bit-shifting.
member.param: The random 64-bit integer.
The number of bits to shift right (0, 8, 16, 24, etc.).
member.returns: A character from the Chars set.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.Block
member.summary: A set of contiguous characters used to build a .
member.remarks:
Example: a mask character of a, Min of 2, and Max of 3, would allow ABC as a valid value.
Example: a mask character of 0, Min of 5, and Max of 7, would allow 09123 as a valid value.
Example: a mask character of *, Min of 1, and Max of 4, would allow B2A7 as a valid value.

The maximum allowed number of characters.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.DateMask
member.summary: An input pattern mask which accepts date values.
member.remarks:
Date masks consist of a combination of: days (e.g. dd), months (e.g. MM), and years (e.g. yy or yyyy), and any delimiter. Custom characters can be used to represent days, months, and years.
Examples: yyyy-MM-dd, MM/dd/yyyy, MM/yy. If custom characters are used, replace the letter blocks with custom characters.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.DateMask.#ctor(System.String,System.Char,System.Char,System.Char)
member.summary: Creates a new date mask.
member.param:
The mask to use. Days must be a two-letter block (e.g. dd), months must be a two-letter block (e.g. MM), and years must be a two- or four-letter block (e.g. yy or yyyy).
If custom characters are used for days, months, or years, change the blocks to use these custom characters.
Examples: yyyy-MM-dd, MM/dd/yyyy, MM/yy.
Defaults to y. The character which represents years.
Defaults to M. The character which represents months.
Defaults to d. The character which represents days.
member.remarks:
Date masks consist of a combination of: days (e.g. dd), months (e.g. MM), and years (e.g. yy or yyyy), and any delimiter.
Examples: yyyy-MM-dd, MM/dd/yyyy, MM/yy. If custom characters are used, replace the letter blocks with custom characters.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.DateMask.ModifyFinalText(System.String)
member.summary: Adjusts the input to be valid for special situations, such as when the last character input results in an invalid date.
member.remarks:
Only takes effect for complete dates.
For example: for mask MM/dd/yyyy, text of 02/29/202_, and a character input of 3, the resulting date 02/29/2023 would be invalid because 2023 is not a leap year.
Another example: for mask MM/dd/yyyy, text of 0_/31/2023, and a character input of 4, the resulting date 04/31/2023 would be invalid because April 31st, 2023 does not exist.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.DateMask.ValidDigitRegularExpression
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^\\d+$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MultiMask
member.summary: A mask which can change its pattern based on partial input.
member.remarks:
A multi-mask consists of multiple values which define when a particular mask is used.
For example: a credit card number can be from any card provider, yet each provider has their own numbering rules. A multi-mask would allow each provider's rules to be used together in a single mask.

member.summary: The currently used mask.
member.remarks: Defaults to null. Changes automatically as the input changes.

member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. Directly equivalent to calling ToString() on .
member.param: The value to retrieve the string value for
member.returns: The string representation of the value, the same as that returned by ToString()

member.name: M:MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.Adornment,MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.SerializationOptions)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. Directly equivalent to calling ToString() on .

The options to use when serializing the enum
member.returns: The string representation of the value, using the provided options.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.Adornment,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. If the member is decorated with the attribute then that value is returned. Otherwise returns uses the name of the member, equivalent to calling ToString() on .
member.param: The value to retrieve the string value for
If uses the value provided in the attribute as the string representation of the member. If , always uses the name of the member, the same as if ToString() was called.
member.returns: The string representation of the value

member.summary: Defines the options use when parsing enums using members provided by NetEscapades.EnumGenerator.

member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets the to use during parsing.
Sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.SerializationOptions
member.summary: Options to apply when calling ToStringFast on an enum.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.SerializationOptions.#ctor(System.Boolean,MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.SerializationTransform)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets whether the value of any metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used in the ToStringFast call.
Sets the to use when serializing the enum value.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AdornmentExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and the value is not transformed.

member.summary: Transform to apply when calling ToStringFast

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AnchorExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AnchorExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AnchorExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AnchorExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.AnchorExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.ButtonTypeExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.ButtonType,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. If the member is decorated with the attribute then that value is returned. Otherwise returns uses the name of the member, equivalent to calling ToString() on .
member.param: The value to retrieve the string value for
If uses the value provided in the attribute as the string representation of the member. If , always uses the name of the member, the same as if ToString() was called.
member.returns: The string representation of the value

member.name: T:MudBlazor.ButtonTypeExtensions.EnumParseOptions
member.summary: Defines the options use when parsing enums using members provided by NetEscapades.EnumGenerator.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.ButtonTypeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.#ctor(System.StringComparison,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets the to use during parsing.
Sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
Sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.ButtonTypeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.ButtonTypeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.ButtonTypeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.ButtonTypeExtensions.SerializationOptions
member.summary: Options to apply when calling ToStringFast on an enum.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.ButtonTypeExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.ButtonTypeExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and the value is not transformed.

member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.EdgeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.EdgeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.InputModeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.InputModeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.InputModeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.InputModeExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.JustifyExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.JustifyExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.

member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. Directly equivalent to calling ToString() on .

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.MaxWidth,MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationOptions)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. Directly equivalent to calling ToString() on .

The options to use when serializing the enum
member.returns: The string representation of the value, using the provided options.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.MaxWidth,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. If the member is decorated with the attribute then that value is returned. Otherwise returns uses the name of the member, equivalent to calling ToString() on .
member.param: The value to retrieve the string value for
If uses the value provided in the attribute as the string representation of the member. If , always uses the name of the member, the same as if ToString() was called.
member.returns: The string representation of the value

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.AsUnderlyingType(MudBlazor.MaxWidth)
member.summary: Cast a value of to the underlying type (int). This is mainly a convenience method.
member.returns: The value of cast to the underlying type.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions
member.summary: Defines the options use when parsing enums using members provided by NetEscapades.EnumGenerator.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions.#ctor(System.StringComparison,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets the to use during parsing.
Sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
Sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationOptions
member.summary: Options to apply when calling ToStringFast on an enum.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationOptions.#ctor(System.Boolean,MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationTransform)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets whether the value of any metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used in the ToStringFast call.
Sets the to use when serializing the enum value.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and the value is not transformed.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationTransform
member.summary: Transform to apply when calling ToStringFast

member.name: F:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationTransform.None
member.summary: Don't apply a transform to the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationTransform.LowerInvariant
member.summary: Call on the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.MaxWidthExtensions.SerializationTransform.UpperInvariant
member.summary: Call on the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.OrientationExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.OrientationExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.OrientationExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.OrientationExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.OrientationExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.

member.param: Sets the to use during parsing.
Sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PositionExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PositionExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PositionExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

Sets the to use when serializing the enum value.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PositionExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.PositionExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and the value is not transformed.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.PositionExtensions.SerializationTransform
member.summary: Transform to apply when calling ToStringFast

member.name: F:MudBlazor.PositionExtensions.SerializationTransform.None
member.summary: Don't apply a transform to the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.SelectionModeExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.SelectionMode,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. If the member is decorated with the attribute then that value is returned. Otherwise returns uses the name of the member, equivalent to calling ToString() on .
member.param: The value to retrieve the string value for
If uses the value provided in the attribute as the string representation of the member. If , always uses the name of the member, the same as if ToString() was called.
member.returns: The string representation of the value

member.name: T:MudBlazor.SelectionModeExtensions.EnumParseOptions
member.summary: Defines the options use when parsing enums using members provided by NetEscapades.EnumGenerator.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.SelectionModeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.#ctor(System.StringComparison,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets the to use during parsing.
Sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
Sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SelectionModeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SelectionModeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SelectionModeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.SelectionModeExtensions.SerializationOptions
member.summary: Options to apply when calling ToStringFast on an enum.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SelectionModeExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SelectionModeExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.

member.summary: Defines the options use when parsing enums using members provided by NetEscapades.EnumGenerator.

member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets the to use during parsing.
Sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SkeletonTypeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SkeletonTypeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SkeletonTypeExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.summary: Options to apply when calling ToStringFast on an enum.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SkeletonTypeExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SkeletonTypeExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and the value is not transformed.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.SkeletonTypeExtensions.SerializationTransform
member.summary: Transform to apply when calling ToStringFast

member.name: F:MudBlazor.SkeletonTypeExtensions.SerializationTransform.None
member.summary: Don't apply a transform to the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.SkeletonTypeExtensions.SerializationTransform.LowerInvariant
member.summary: Call on the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.SkeletonTypeExtensions.SerializationTransform.UpperInvariant
member.summary: Call on the ToStringFast() result.

member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SortDirectionExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SortDirectionExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.SortDirectionExtensions.SerializationOptions
member.summary: Options to apply when calling ToStringFast on an enum.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.SortDirectionExtensions.SerializationOptions.#ctor(System.Boolean,MudBlazor.SortDirectionExtensions.SerializationTransform)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets whether the value of any metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used in the ToStringFast call.
Sets the to use when serializing the enum value.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SortDirectionExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.SortDirectionExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and the value is not transformed.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.SortDirectionExtensions.SerializationTransform
member.summary: Transform to apply when calling ToStringFast

member.name: P:MudBlazor.TypoExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.TypoExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. Directly equivalent to calling ToString() on .

member.returns: The string representation of the value, the same as that returned by ToString()

member.name: M:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.Variant,MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationOptions)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. Directly equivalent to calling ToString() on .

The options to use when serializing the enum
member.returns: The string representation of the value, using the provided options.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.Variant,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. If the member is decorated with the attribute then that value is returned. Otherwise returns uses the name of the member, equivalent to calling ToString() on .
member.param: The value to retrieve the string value for
If uses the value provided in the attribute as the string representation of the member. If , always uses the name of the member, the same as if ToString() was called.
member.returns: The string representation of the value

member.name: M:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.AsUnderlyingType(MudBlazor.Variant)
member.summary: Cast a value of to the underlying type (int). This is mainly a convenience method.
member.returns: The value of cast to the underlying type.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.EnumParseOptions
member.summary: Defines the options use when parsing enums using members provided by NetEscapades.EnumGenerator.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.EnumParseOptions.#ctor(System.StringComparison,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets the to use during parsing.
Sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
Sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationOptions
member.summary: Options to apply when calling ToStringFast on an enum.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationOptions.#ctor(System.Boolean,MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationTransform)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets whether the value of any metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used in the ToStringFast call.
Sets the to use when serializing the enum value.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and the value is not transformed.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationTransform
member.summary: Transform to apply when calling ToStringFast

member.name: F:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationTransform.None
member.summary: Don't apply a transform to the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationTransform.LowerInvariant
member.summary: Call on the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.VariantExtensions.SerializationTransform.UpperInvariant
member.summary: Call on the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.Length
member.summary: The number of members in the enum. This is a non-distinct count of defined names.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.Width)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. Directly equivalent to calling ToString() on .
member.param: The value to retrieve the string value for
member.returns: The string representation of the value, the same as that returned by ToString()

member.name: M:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.Width,MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationOptions)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. Directly equivalent to calling ToString() on .
member.param: The value to retrieve the string value for
The options to use when serializing the enum
member.returns: The string representation of the value, using the provided options.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.ToStringFast(MudBlazor.Width,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the value. If the member is decorated with the attribute then that value is returned. Otherwise returns uses the name of the member, equivalent to calling ToString() on .
member.param: The value to retrieve the string value for
If uses the value provided in the attribute as the string representation of the member. If , always uses the name of the member, the same as if ToString() was called.
member.returns: The string representation of the value

member.name: M:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.AsUnderlyingType(MudBlazor.Width)
member.summary: Cast a value of to the underlying type (int). This is mainly a convenience method.
member.returns: The value of cast to the underlying type.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions
member.summary: Defines the options use when parsing enums using members provided by NetEscapades.EnumGenerator.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions.#ctor(System.StringComparison,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets the to use during parsing.
Sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
Sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions.ComparisonType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and a case-sensitive comparison will be used.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of any metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored. Set to to enable parsing using the applicable MetadataSource for each enum member.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.EnumParseOptions.EnableNumberParsing
member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and numbers will be parsed as well as names.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationOptions
member.summary: Options to apply when calling ToStringFast on an enum.

member.name: M:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationOptions.#ctor(System.Boolean,MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationTransform)
member.summary: Create an instance of
member.param: Sets whether the value of any metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used in the ToStringFast call.
Sets the to use when serializing the enum value.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationOptions.UseMetadataAttributes
member.summary: Gets whether the value of any metadata attributes applied to an enum member should be used as the ToString() value for the enum.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , so the value of metadata attributes on the enum values are ignored.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationOptions.Transform
member.summary: Gets the to use during parsing.
member.remarks: By default, it's set to , and the value is not transformed.

member.name: T:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationTransform
member.summary: Transform to apply when calling ToStringFast

member.name: F:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationTransform.None
member.summary: Don't apply a transform to the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationTransform.LowerInvariant
member.summary: Call on the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: F:MudBlazor.WidthExtensions.SerializationTransform.UpperInvariant
member.summary: Call on the ToStringFast() result.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.BreakpointExtensions.EnumParseOptions.AllowMatchingMetadataAttribute
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the value of the selected metadata value attribute values applied to an enum should be used as the parse value for an enum.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value defining whether numbers should be parsed as a fallback when other parsing fails.

member.name: P:MudBlazor.MudContainer.Fixed
member.summary: Set the max-width to match the min-width of the current breakpoint. This is useful if you'd prefer to design for a fixed set of sizes instead of trying to accommodate a fully fluid viewport. It's fluid by default.
